title: paeonia anomala subsp. pamiroalaica
intro: Paeonia anomala subsp. pamiroalaica, also known as the Pamir peony or the Himalayan peony, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Paeonia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Paeonia anomala subsp. pamiroalaica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat gastrointestinal problems. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
